anchor_seed.name: Anchor Seed
anchor_seed.tagline: Entry-level managed infrastructure on hardware you already own
anchor_seed.summary: A minimal but professionally managed infrastructure deployment on your existing hardware — Raspberry Pi, repurposed laptop, or similar. You gain encrypted backups, DNS filtering, a self-hosted password manager, availability monitoring, and a clear upgrade path — without purchasing new equipment.
included: Docker Compose deployment on your existing hardware (Raspberry Pi, laptop, or similar)
Security baseline: hardened OS, key-based SSH, fail2ban, firewall default-deny, automatic security updates
Pi-hole: DNS filtering and local DNS resolution
Vaultwarden: self-hosted password manager (Bitwarden-compatible)
Automated daily backups with database-safe methods to local storage (USB drive or second partition)
Uptime Kuma: service availability monitoring
Tailscale: secure remote admin access (no public port exposure)
System baseline document: service inventory, access model, recovery procedures
Automated health check script tailored to your deployment
not_included: Hypervisor (Proxmox) — Docker Compose provides service isolation at this tier
Reverse proxy with wildcard TLS (services accessed via IP:port or Tailscale hostname)
SIEM (Wazuh) — resource requirements exceed Seed-class hardware
Offsite or off-host replication (backup is local only)
Grafana dashboards or detailed metrics collection
Monthly manual patching (automated security updates handle this)

anchor_node.name: Anchor Node
anchor_node.tagline: Single-site infrastructure baseline with monitoring, backups, and security hardening
anchor_node.summary: A documented, hardened, monitored, and backed-up infrastructure deployment. You gain a clear picture of what you have, confidence that backups work, and a defined point of contact for infrastructure issues.
included: Security baseline: hardened OS, key-based SSH, fail2ban, firewall default-deny, TLS on all public services, encrypted storage
Automated daily backups with database-appropriate methods and defined retention
Initial backup restore test performed and documented
Host-level monitoring: CPU, memory, disk, network for all managed systems
Service availability monitoring with alerting
Backup health monitoring via push-based checks
System baseline document: network diagram, service inventory, access model, credential locations
Change control: all changes logged with rollback plans
Monthly patching within agreed maintenance window
Critical CVE patches within 72 hours
not_included: Formal DR drills (backup restore tests are included; full DR simulations are not)

anchor_hybrid.name: Anchor Hybrid
anchor_hybrid.tagline: On-premises infrastructure plus offsite replication with documented DR procedures
anchor_hybrid.summary: Everything in Anchor Node, plus geographic redundancy, a tested recovery plan, and reduced risk from site-level events. You gain protection against hardware failure, theft, fire, and flood — with documented evidence.
included: Everything in Anchor Node
Automated offsite data replication to a geographically separate location
Disaster recovery procedures documented for each critical service
Semiannual restore from offsite backup, documented with timestamps and results
Cross-site replication status monitoring and health checks
SIEM deployment with file integrity monitoring and authentication correlation
Quarterly infrastructure reviews: backup health, patch compliance, open items
not_included: Full DR simulations with client participation (available as add-on)

anchor_enterprise.name: Anchor Enterprise
anchor_enterprise.tagline: Full governance model with high availability, formal DR drills, and structured reporting
anchor_enterprise.summary: Full operational governance for organizations that need high availability, formal change control, regular DR drills, and structured reporting. Suitable for compliance requirements, higher uptime needs, or multiple critical services.
included: Everything in Anchor Hybrid
High-availability failover configurations for critical services
Formal change advisory process with documented approval chain
Quarterly DR drills with client participation and drill reports
Monthly reporting: uptime, incidents, patching, backup status, open items
Annual infrastructure-wide security review with findings report
Application-level health checks, certificate expiry tracking, capacity trend analysis
Defined incident escalation paths with after-hours coverage
not_included: Compliance certification (we prepare, we do not certify)
Penetration testing (we refer to qualified firms)

sla.maintenance_window: Agreed weekly with emergency exception process
